papers.title_en: MSTF-Net: A UAV-Oriented Multi-Spectral Video Segmentation Method via Modality-Robust, Scale-Adaptive, and Consistent Fusion
papers.authors: Chenwei Wang, Zhida Wang, Zelin Li, Elif Ozden-Yenigun, Houde Liu
papers.evidence: 面向UAV遥感的多光谱（RGB和热）融合分割
papers.tldr: 无人机多光谱视频分割面临RGB与热模态差异导致的融合困境以及平台运动引起的时序不一致问题。为此提出MSTF-Net，通过MSCSE模块利用跨模态注意力抑制模态特异性噪声，MTCSC模块自适应调整时域感受野保持语义一致性。在MVSeg和CART数据集上分别达到56.42%和51.80% mIoU，显著优于现有方法。该框架有效融合了跨模态互补信息与时序动态，特别适用于小目标、遮挡及模态退化等挑战场景。
papers.abstract_en: Multi-spectral video segmentation is essential for robust scene understanding in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) applications such as city planning, land use monitoring, traffic monitoring, and crowd estimation. While the fusion of RGB and thermal modalities offers complementary information for perception under varying lighting and visibility conditions, two fundamental challenges remain: (1) the modal fusion dilemma, arising from significant discrepancies between RGB and thermal features that obscure complementary cues, and (2) temporal variation, induced by rapid motion and viewpoint changes on UAV platforms, which leads to appearance inconsistency and misalignment across frames. To address these issues, this study proposed MSTF-Net, a modality-robust scale-adaptive fusion framework for multi-spectral video segmentation that effectively models cross-modal fusion and temporal consistency. The Modality Spatial Complementary Suppression and Enhancement (MSCSE) module generates unified instance queries via cross-modal attention and suppresses modality-specific noise using residual-guided discrepancy filtering and consistency constraints. To model temporal dynamics, the Multi-scale Temporal Cross-modality Semantic Consistency (MTCSC) module adaptively adjusts the temporal receptive field based on frame distance, capturing both coarse global context and fine local structure across time. Extensive ablation experiments on public RGB-T datasets demonstrate that MSTF-Net achieves state-of-the-art segmentation performance, especially under challenging conditions such as small targets, occlusion, and modality degradation. Specifically, reached 56.42\% mIoU on the MVSeg dataset and 51.80\% mIoU on the CART dataset, respectively.

papers.title_en: More with Less: a Large Scale Remote Sensing VLM with a Simple Recipe
papers.authors: Stefan Maria Ailuro, Mario Markov, Mohammad Mahdi, Luc Van Gool, Danda Pani Paudel
papers.evidence: 大规模遥感视觉-语言模型用于多模态融合
papers.tldr: 遥感视觉语言模型常依赖专门架构设计，本文证明通用VLM在足够大规模多样化数据训练下即可达到竞争性能。模型采用单一语言策略，通过多任务强化学习统一回答文本或调用定位工具，覆盖VQA、描述、检测、分割等任务。在多种遥感基准上取得竞争性甚至最优结果，且数据规模增加带来一致提升。结论表明，数据规模比架构创新对遥感VLM更为关键。
papers.abstract_en: Remote sensing vision-language models are increasingly expected to support open-ended reasoning over Earth Observation data and a variety of tasks. Most recent progress in this area has been driven by remote-sensing-specific architectural designs, often introducing new encoders, alignment modules, or task-specific fusion mechanisms. In this work, we challenge the necessity of such architectural specialization. We show that a generally capable vision-language model can achieve competitive or state-of-the-art performance at challenging remote sensing benchmarks, provided that it is trained at sufficient scale across diverse data and tasks. Our model uses a single language policy that can either answer directly in text or invoke a localization tool for segmentation and grounding. To train this heterogeneous behaviour, we employ a multi-task reinforcement learning framework with adaptive task rewards covering multiple-choice VQA, free-form VQA, captioning, detection, and segmentation across a large variety of input types. Our approach achieves competitive results across a broad set of benchmarks, including high-resolution, multi-temporal, multi-modal and multi-view tasks. Further, as training data scales, our experiments show consistent improvements across most tasks both in and out of distribution, which correlate with per-task data diversity. These findings suggest that, for remote sensing VLMs, data scale is more important than architectural novelty.

papers.title_en: On the modality gap and the contrastive loss in multi-modal representation learning
papers.authors: Fabian Mager, Hiba Nassar, Lars Kai Hansen
papers.evidence: 多模态表示学习与模态差距分析
papers.tldr: 针对CLIP风格双编码器对比学习中的模态差距问题，本文通过实验和理论分析指出，InfoNCE损失在低温下强制模态分离是导致差距的主要原因。为此提出xNCE损失，引入模态内负样本对，在不牺牲判别几何的前提下显著缩小模态差距。xNCE在MS-COCO上保持检索性能，并在所有零样本分类基准上超过InfoNCE，证明了其有效性。
papers.abstract_en: We study the modality gap in CLIP-style dual-encoder contrastive learning, where image and text embeddings remain misaligned despite being trained in a shared space. We argue that the gap is induced by a failure of the InfoNCE formulation with independent encoders. We conduct a uni-modal experiment with two independent encoders and identical initialization conditions and find that InfoNCE actively generates a gap at low temperatures. We provide a theoretical analysis of this phenomenon and show that the modality gap is indeed a mode-failure of InfoNCE, but only at low temperatures. We propose a simple modification called xNCE, which uses intermodal as well as intra-modality negative contrastive pairs. xNCE matches retrieval performance on MS-COCO while consistently reducing the gap even at low temperatures. Notably, xNCE improves zero-shot classification over the InfoNCE baseline across all benchmarks, whereas high-temperature InfoNCE and regularized InfoNCE both fail to do so, demonstrating that xNCE reduces the modality gap without sacrificing the discriminative geometry needed for transfer.

papers.title_en: TabPFN beyond Tabular Data: Calibration and Accuracy on Multimodal Embeddings
papers.authors: Jingxiang Zhang, Lujia Zhong, Zijie Zhu, Shuo Huang, Yuang Xu
papers.evidence: 多模态嵌入分类头，可应用于遥感多模态分类
papers.tldr: 少样本多模态分类常采用轻量分类头但校准不佳。本文将TabPFN作为即插即用零梯度分类头，用于冻结的图像、文本、音频编码器。在14个数据集、11个编码器和三个模态的22820个评估episode上，TabPFN在负对数似然和期望校准误差上取得最佳平均排名，分别降低48-62%和2.1-5.3倍，同时保持或超越平均准确率。其优势在中等至高样本数、低维特征时显著，并在骨干网络适应中改善校准。该工作为校准敏感的多模态分类提供了免训练头的经验指导。
papers.abstract_en: Few-shot multimodal classification commonly attaches a lightweight head, such as $k$-nearest neighbors, logistic regression, or a linear SVM, to a frozen pretrained encoder. Although computationally efficient, these heads can produce poorly calibrated confidence scores, limiting their reliability in calibration-sensitive applications. We evaluate TabPFN as a plug-and-play, zero-gradient classification head for frozen image, text, and audio encoders. Across 22{,}820 evaluation episodes spanning 14 datasets, 11 encoders, and three modalities, TabPFN achieves the best mean rank among nine classification heads on both negative log-likelihood (NLL) and expected calibration error (ECE). At a representative setting, it reduces NLL by 48--62\% and ECE by 2.1--5.3$\times$ relative to the average of the eight baselines while matching or exceeding their average accuracy. Its accuracy advantage is conditional, concentrating at moderate-to-high shot counts and low-to-moderate feature dimensions ($k \ge 50$, $d \le 32$), and diminishing when labeled data are scarce, feature dimensions are high, or competing methods approach ceiling accuracy. In targeted backbone-adaptation experiments, replacing the trained linear head with TabPFN substantially improves calibration while preserving competitive accuracy. These results provide empirical guidance for using TabPFN as a training-free head in calibration-sensitive multimodal classification. To support transparency and reproducibility, we publicly release the source code, experiment configurations, and evaluation scripts in our GitHub repository: https://github.com/Jingxiang-Zhang/tabpfn-multimodal-embeddings.
